Title: An engineered combinatorial module of transcription factors boosts production of monoterpenoid indole alkaloids in Catharanthus roseus

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: bHLH transcription factor; jasmonate-responsive MYC2 ortholog
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH clade IIIe; contains JID domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related specialized metabolites; includes pharmaceutically valuable MIAs such as vinblastine/vincristine pathway intermediates
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flower petals in this study; broader MIA accumulation in aerial tissues and roots depending on branch
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type CrMYC2a has limited activation capacity in planta/protoplasts due to JAZ repression; it weakly induces BIS1/BIS2 and some iridoid genes but does not significantly increase MIA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMYC2a is positioned upstream in the jasmonate-regulated MIA network. 2. Wild-type CrMYC2a failed to strongly transactivate most tested MIA promoters in tobacco protoplasts, unlike its de-repressed mutant. 3. In petals, CrMYC2a overexpression induced BIS1/BIS2 and a few iridoid genes modestly but did not significantly alter major MIA levels.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in C. roseus flower petals; tobacco BY-2 protoplast transactivation assays; qPCR; LC-FT-ICR-MS metabolite profiling; yeast one-hybrid context from prior work
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate signaling; JAZ repressors; wounding/insect feeding/JA elicitation discussed as upstream cues
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation, but normally constrained by JAZ-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of iridoid and MIA biosynthesis and associated transport
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BIS1; BIS2; weak indirect induction of GES; 8HGO; IS under overexpression conditions
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Weakly activates BIS genes and indirectly some IPAP-specific iridoid genes; little effect on late MIA genes in wild-type form
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream jasmonate-responsive regulator, but wild-type protein is not sufficient alone for strong MIA boosting under the tested conditions because negative repression limits activity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient overexpression in flower petals; tobacco protoplast assay context; endogenous JA signaling in protoplasts likely relevant
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; tested against pBIS1, pORCA3, pSTR and multiple MIA gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CrJAZ1; CrJAZ2; CrJAZ3; CrJAZ4; CrJAZ7; CrJAZ8
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in positive feedforward/amplification by inducing BIS and ORCA TFs; proposed negative feedback via induction of JAZ repressors
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as first direct regulator of SGD expression according to discussion; direct regulation supported strongly by mutant transactivation data, while wild-type activity is masked by repression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive activation of MYC2 module may alter JA signaling, growth, and cause defense-compound overaccumulation; stable overexpression may be deleterious
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream regulator, but de-repressed engineering is more effective than wild type for metabolic engineering; should likely be combined with BIS1 and ORCA3

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Engineered de-repressed bHLH transcription factor; MYC2 mutant
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Strictosidine; hörhammericine; 16-methoxyhörhammericine; 19-hydroxytabersonine; broad monoterpenoid indole alkaloid set
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related specialized metabolites and medically relevant alkaloid pathway products
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly activates iridoid/MIA pathway genes and increases strictosidine about fivefold; in combination with BIS1 and ORCA3 causes broad MIA accumulation including dramatic induction of root-branch MIAs.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMYC2aD126N transactivated pBIS1, pORCA3, pSTR, and multiple MIA pathway promoters. 2. In petals it strongly induced BIS, ORCA, transporter, iridoid, seco-iridoid, and early MIA genes, but not vindoline/catharanthine branch genes. 3. It increased strictosidine accumulation and, with BIS1+ORCA3, synergistically boosted many MIAs including root-specific branches.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Engineered mutagenesis; tobacco BY-2 protoplast transactivation assays; yeast two-hybrid interaction assays with JAZ proteins; transient overexpression in C. roseus petals; qPCR; LC-FT-ICR-MS metabolomics; PCA
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Engineered to bypass normal JAZ repression in jasmonate signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Constitutive/de-repressed transcriptional activation due to weakened JAZ interaction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of iridoid biosynthesis, strictosidine formation, downstream early MIA branching, and transporter expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BIS1; BIS2; ORCA3; ORCA5; GES; G8O; 8HGO; IS; IO; SLS; TDC; SGD; STR; LAMT; HYS; GO; T19H; MAT; TAT; NPF2.5; NPF2.6; NPF2.9
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates numerous MIA pathway genes and transporters; strong direct/indirect induction of BIS and ORCA regulators; synergizes with ORCA3 especially on GES, 8HGO, G8O, IS, IO, GS2, T19H, MAT and TAT
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key engineered switch that is necessary and sufficient for strong strictosidine increase and is the main enabling component of the superior triple-TF module
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration in petals; protoplast system; no exogenous JA required because mutant is de-repressed
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CrJAZ1; CrJAZ2; CrJAZ4; CrJAZ7; reduced/lost interaction with CrJAZ3; CrJAZ8
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in/escapes the JAZ-MYC2 repression complex
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Disrupts negative feedback through JAZ repression; activates feedforward amplification through BIS and ORCA TF cascades
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not induce vindoline pathway genes, Redox1/2, SAT, HL1, HL2, indicating additional unknown regulators are needed for full vinblastine branch activation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential growth/development penalties and constitutive defense metabolite overaccumulation if stably expressed
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potent metabolic-engineering tool; should be stacked with BIS1 and ORCA3, preferably under inducible control to minimize pleiotropy

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Strictosidine and upstream iridoid precursors within MIA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly upregulates IPAP-specific iridoid pathway genes and BIS2 but alone does not increase strictosidine or broader abundant MIAs in petals.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS1 specifically activates IPAP-specific iridoid genes. 2. It strongly induces BIS2, forming an amplification loop. 3. Alone it is insufficient to boost major MIA accumulation, but contributes to the triple-TF module.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in flower petals; qPCR; combinatorial co-expression; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate-inducible network; induced by CrMYC2aD126N and weakly by ORCA3
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Iridoid precursor biosynthesis in the MIA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Strong activation of iridoid pathway genes upstream of 7DLH; no direct activation of late MIA genes reported
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Branch-specific regulator for the iridoid arm supplying MIA precursors, but not sufficient alone for large MIA output
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration transient expression in flower petals
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA-inducible according to prior studies discussed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive amplification loop via BIS2 induction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not interact with tested JAZ proteins in prior assays cited/discussed; lacks typical JID domain
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Limited standalone metabolic output despite strong transcript induction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a component of combinatorial TF cassettes with CrMYC2aD126N and ORCA3

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related and medicinal alkaloid pathway metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induces seco-iridoid/MIA pathway genes downstream of 7DLH and some transporter genes; alone does not increase strictosidine, but synergizes strongly with CrMYC2aD126N and contributes to broad MIA accumulation in the triple module.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA3 activates downstream seco-iridoid and MIA genes and transporters. 2. ORCA3 alone shows limited metabolite output in petals. 3. ORCA3 acts synergistically with CrMYC2aD126N, and with BIS1 in the triple combination, to maximize MIA pathway induction and alkaloid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in petals; qPCR; combinatorial co-expression; metabolite profiling; promoter transactivation context discussed
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Downstream seco-iridoid and MIA biosynthesis plus transporter expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes downstream of 7DLH; NPF2.6; NPF2.9; GS1; GS2; T19H; MAT; and indirectly BIS1/BIS2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates downstream MIA branch genes; weaker indirect induction of several iridoid genes and BIS genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important downstream branch regulator whose strongest value emerges in synergy with de-repressed MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration transient expression in flower petals
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA-inducible based on prior work discussed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible auto-amplification loop; minor endogenous ORCA3 induction by ORCA3 overexpression; participates in feedforward cascade from MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Associated with increased accumulation of a putative flavonoid isomer of rhamnetin 3-rutinoside, suggesting some off-target metabolic effect
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May perturb non-MIA flavonoid metabolism; alone gives limited desired MIA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key combinatorial component for engineering broad MIA output, especially with CrMYC2aD126N and BIS1

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Engineered de-repressed heterologous bHLH transcription factor; MYC2 mutant
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related and medicinal alkaloid pathway products
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous de-repressed MYC2 strongly induces MIA pathway genes and increases strictosidine in C. roseus petals similarly to CrMYC2aD126N.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYC2D105N transactivated pBIS1, pORCA3, pSTR and promoted MIA gene expression. 2. In petals it caused induction patterns similar to CrMYC2aD126N. 3. It increased strictosidine accumulation, showing transferability of the de-repression strategy across species.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protoplast transactivation assays; Y2H with CrJAZ proteins; transient overexpression in C. roseus petals; qPCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of iridoid/MIA biosynthesis in heterologous context
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BIS genes; ORCA genes; MIA pathway promoters including STR and others
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates broad MIA-pathway transcription in C. roseus
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Demonstrates that de-repressed MYC2 engineering is portable and sufficient to enhance strictosidine accumulation in a heterologous species context
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration in C. roseus petals; tobacco protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CrJAZ4; CrJAZ7; reduced/lost interaction with CrJAZ1; CrJAZ2; CrJAZ3; CrJAZ8
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as proof-of-principle control demonstrating conservation of MYC2 de-repression mechanism
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Same conceptual risks as de-repressed MYC2s: growth and defense-metabolism pleiotropy under constitutive expression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports use of engineered 'super-MYC2s' across species for metabolic engineering
